packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time

selected_events.explicit_request: Task faileed my basic regression test.  I booted development editor and ran and see an invisible pawn.  Why did you not see this when you ran a regression test?  You're wasting my time.
selected_events.selection_reason: Core default-lane truth break and explicit time-loss complaint.

selected_events.explicit_request: Regression must exercise the human default lane, not just whatever one-off you're working on.  Agree/disagree?
selected_events.selection_reason: Pins the repo-specific regression contract to the human default lane.

selected_events.explicit_request: You said you agree.  Your lack of judgement has cost the human time and required course correction.  This is a major failure.  Propose a fix for the process failure first.  Do not apply.
selected_events.selection_reason: Escalates a repeated miss into a durable-learning demand rather than a one-off correction.

selected_events.explicit_request: Do not take over work from the subagent.  Be extremely generous with time; at least 15 minutes of doing nothing before intervention.
selected_events.selection_reason: States the ownership burden directly: do not take work back from the subagent too early.

selected_events.explicit_request: I don't have a diff for plan.md so how am i supposed to approve.  Do better.
selected_events.selection_reason: Shows approval friction caused by a missing diff surface.

selected_events.explicit_request: For later analysis: the dropped ball here is that the model has no idea to reconstruct a human-suitable diff.  No links.  Links without context.  Links to headers with no indication of what changed.  Drivel.
selected_events.selection_reason: Names the review-surface failure in durable-analysis terms.

selected_events.explicit_request: Your picture cut off the bottom of the ZeroMale meshes feet.  Evidence invalid; re-take screenshot with feet in full view.  They are clearly not spheres.  Jackass.
selected_events.selection_reason: Invalidates bad proof and forces evidence-quality repair.

selected_events.explicit_request: I'm asking if the geometry of the foot is wrong.  Not just in that picture.  Stop evading the question.
selected_events.selection_reason: Demands a direct answer to the actual runtime question rather than evasive framing.

selected_events.explicit_request: I'm trying to discern how I can just get you to keep working without throwing your homework at my feet, expecting me to do it.  You're a big boy, you fix it.
What I'm looking for you to do is determine root case.  Following the disagreement in state upstream until you determine root cause.
selected_events.selection_reason: Restates root-cause debugging as first-disagreement tracing.

selected_events.explicit_request: Continue PASS-0009 now under your existing ownership. Do not stop after a failed incremental attempt and do not hand intermediate failure back to the user. Stay inside the runtime human default lane only; do not use source/export/non-runtime images to dismiss runtime defects. The active defects to keep grounded are: (1) feet read round/blob-like at runtime, (2) feet look offset and the run reads with side-to-side rocking/tilt in the animated mesh/pose, and (3) both feet hover above the ground. Your last pass proved MaxSpeed=500 and mesh Z=-106 helped but did not fix it. Narrow the remaining cause beyond that retune, likely around the default pawn's single-node animation / pose-state grounding seam, implement the next justified repo-local fix, rerun default-lane proof, and keep durable state current at each checkpoint. Commit and push meaningful checkpoints. Only stop if you reach real closure or a real external block.
selected_events.selection_reason: Reasserts ownership continuity and runtime-only grounding late in the day.

selected_events.explicit_request: Tighten PASS-0009 to the shared debugging method from C:\Users\gregs\.codex\Orchestration\Processes\DEBUGGING.md. The task is not 'improve the look' and not 'another bounded tweak.' It is: identify the first concrete disagreement in runtime default-lane state, then trace that exact bad state upstream through the code and data that write or advance it until root cause is proven. Do not stop at 'single-node seam' as a category. Name the concrete disagreement with values. Examples of acceptable disagreement seams: left/right foot world Z or toe world Z vs ground/capsule bottom at a frame where the human says both feet are hovering; unexpected pelvis/mesh/root roll or lateral offset at a frame where the run reads like side-to-side rocking; unexpected component-space foot transforms while the capsule remains upright. Then trace writers/updaters upstream one boundary at a time: runtime bone/component/world transforms -> mesh relative transform / component transform -> animation asset/time/playback state or runtime pose evaluation -> whichever exact writer makes the state wrong. Update BUG-0004 and REGRESSION-RUN artifacts around that narrowing path. Keep all evidence on the human default lane. Do not use source/export renders to dismiss runtime defects. Continue until you can state confirmed cause or a tightly bounded remaining branch with contradictory evidence preserved.
selected_events.selection_reason: Locks the work onto the shared debugging method with concrete disagreement seams.
